alignments.candidate_evidence: Proficient in SQL with joins, GROUP BY, subqueries/CTEs, window functions; queries BigQuery directly to pull and shape own data
alignments.job_requirement: Excellent SQL and data transformation skills (e.g. ideally proficient in dbt or similar)

alignments.candidate_evidence: Builds Looker dashboards; experience translating business and product requirements into analytics structures
alignments.job_requirement: Design and implement Looker structures (explores, views, etc) which will enable users across the organization to self-serve analytics

alignments.candidate_evidence: Working proficiency with BigQuery (Google Cloud's data warehouse); eight years at Google with internal data stack experience
alignments.job_requirement: Familiarity with at least one of these Cloud technologies: Snowflake, AWS, Google Cloud, Microsoft Azure

alignments.candidate_evidence: Eight years translating commercial, product, and transformation requirements into data, metrics, and reporting cadences; deep stakeholder and product team collaboration
alignments.job_requirement: Work closely with data analysts and business teams to understand business requirements and provide data ready for analysis and reporting

alignments.candidate_evidence: Commercial operations and revenue operations background; built forecasting, territory budgeting, and pricing analytics
alignments.job_requirement: Passionate about sales, finance, customer, marketing and/or product analytics data

gaps.gap: dbt proficiency required; candidate has SQL and BigQuery but no stated dbt or production data-transformation framework experience
gaps.mitigation: dbt is learnable for someone with strong SQL and data-warehouse fundamentals; onboarding and mentorship on dbt patterns would be needed

gaps.gap: Data modeling at scale in large production environments not explicitly demonstrated; experience is analytics and operations-focused rather than data-engineering infrastructure
gaps.mitigation: Candidate has schema, metric, and reporting design experience but would need structured ramp-up on enterprise data-warehouse modeling patterns

gaps.gap: No stated experience with version control, testing, or CI/CD in analytics engineering; these are core to the role
gaps.mitigation: Candidate has worked with Salesforce feature requests and system integrations but SDLC rigor in analytics context is not evidenced

gaps.gap: Snowflake is the stated tool; candidate knows BigQuery. Cloud warehouses have syntax and feature differences, though fundamentals transfer
gaps.mitigation: Snowflake and BigQuery are similar enough that SQL and modeling skills transfer quickly; tool-specific training is standard

output.level_rationale: Role requires hands-on data engineering, modeling, and SDLC rigor with no management scope or years stated explicitly. Title "Analytics Engineer" in a fintech scale-up context, paired with end-to-end data-product ownership and cloud-warehouse infrastructure, suggests mid-level IC (L4). No evidence of senior/principal scope or management-of-managers. company stage not stated, reducing confidence.
output.summary: Candidate has strong SQL, BigQuery, Looker, and stakeholder-collaboration skills aligned to several core requirements. Critical gaps: no dbt experience (role specifies ideal proficiency), no large-scale data-modeling portfolio, and no stated SDLC/testing/CI-CD rigor in analytics. This is a specialized analytics-engineering role requiring data-infrastructure and SDLC depth; candidate's background is commercial-operations and product-requirement translation, not production data engineering. Skills are partially overlapping but the depth and focus differ materially. Role does not belong to target or approved-stretch families.
uncertainties: dbt experience not mentioned; critical gap but may be learnable
Candidate's data-modeling experience scope in large-scale environments not detailed; inferred from operations/analytics context
Snowflake vs. BigQuery syntax and features; transfer feasibility depends on depth of Snowflake-specific patterns
Company stage, team size, and SDLC maturity at Checkout.com not stated in JD; affects role level and onboarding investment
